reasoning: With only 24 days remaining between the knowledge cutoff (June 6, 2025) and resolution date (July 1, 2025), the probability of an official cancellation announcement is low. While Bulgargaz has ceased payments since July 2024 and is effectively bankrupt, and political leaders (Borissov) are actively pushing for an exit or amendment (May 2025), a unilateral cancellation by Bulgaria would trigger a massive BGN 3–4 billion penalty due to the 'take-or-pay' clause. Consequently, they are prioritizing renegotiation, and BOTAŞ has offered counter-proposals. Though Bulgargaz has hired a Swiss law firm to evaluate termination options, international contract disputes and negotiations typically drag on. BOTAŞ is unlikely to hastily terminate while high-level political talks and commercial renegotiations are ongoing, and Bulgaria is constrained by the financial ruin a unilateral termination would cause. Given the extremely narrow timeframe, an explicit cancellation before July 1 is unlikely.
